response: Alex Rodriguez won two of the Commissioner's Historic Achievement Award, which is presented by the Commissioner of Baseball. The award is presented by the Commissioner of Baseball to a group or person who has made a "major impact on the sport" of baseball. The award has been presented fourteen times by Commissioner Bud Selig: eleven times to players, once to a team, and twice to a non-player. The most recent recipient is Derek Jeter (2014), who was honored in the final year of his career for being "one of the most accomplished shortstops of all-time." The award is not an annual award but is presented at the Commissioner's discretion.
